title: Hype & Trending Shopping Area Signal
description: TinyFish Layer 1 schema — captures real-time signals indicating a geographic area is trending as a desirable retail destination. Sources: social media buzz, news coverage of 'hot neighborhoods', foot traffic surge events, and sales promotions that are driving consumer attention to a specific area right now.
_tinyfish_agent_goal: You are analyzing whether a specific area is currently trending as a desirable shopping destination. Look for: (1) News or blog articles from the past 90 days calling this area 'up and coming', 'hottest neighborhood', 'best new shopping district', or similar. (2) Any major sales events (grand opening sales, pop-up markets, weekend retail events) that are drawing crowds to this area RIGHT NOW. (3) Social proof signals — are there TikTok/Instagram/Yelp posts about this area going viral? (4) New anchor tenants or flagship stores that signal the area is becoming a retail destination (e.g., Apple, Lululemon, Nike opening here = area validation). Extract and return structured signals only — no speculation.
_tinyfish_search_queries: "[CITY]" "hottest shopping" OR "up and coming" OR "best new shops" site:timeout.com OR site:eater.com OR site:curbed.com 2024 2025
"[CITY]" "[NEIGHBORHOOD]" new store opening grand opening sale 2025
"[CITY]" retail development "coming soon" OR "now open" OR "opening this year" 2025

area_hype_score.description: 0=dead zone, 100=this area is the hottest retail destination right now. Score 70+ if there are multiple independent sources naming this area as trending.

trending_momentum.description: Direction of trend: surging = multiple signals in last 30 days, growing = positive but slower, stable = no change, declining = areas losing retail relevance.

anchor_store_openings.description: Major brand or anchor store recent openings that validate the area as a retail destination. A new Target, Whole Foods, or Apple Store opening nearby is a strong co-tenancy signal.

sale_events.description: Active or upcoming retail sales events, pop-up markets, or promotional events driving foot traffic to this area right now.

media_coverage.description: Recent media articles (news, lifestyle, food/retail blogs) naming this area as trending.

signals.description: All detected hype signals in ranked order (strongest first).

recency_days.description: How many days ago this signal was published/posted

narrative.description: 1–3 sentence plain-English summary: is this area hot right now, and WHY? Mention specific evidence (e.g. 'Three new flagship stores opened in Q1 2025, and local media named it Phoenix's fastest-growing retail corridor').
